opinion.h: BARGAINTOWN, U.S. A., INC., ET AL. v. WHITMAN, DISTRICT ATTORNEY OF LEBANON COUNTY PA., ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE MIDDLE DISTRICT OF
opinion.p: No. 76. Decided June 19, 1961. ___ F. Supp. ___, affirmed.
David G. Bress and Leonard Braman for appellants.
Anne X. Alpern, Attorney General of Pennsylvania, and Harry J. Rubin for appellees.
The judgment is affirmed. McGowan v. Maryland, 366 U.S. 420, and Two Guys from Harrison-Allentown, Inc., v. McGinley, 366 U.S. 582.
MR. JUSTICE DOUGLAS is of the opinion that probable jurisdiction should be noted.
367 U.S. 903 BECK v. MAINE. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME JUDICIAL COURT OF MAINE. No. 899. Decided June 19, 1961.
Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
Frank E. Hancock, Attorney General of Maine, for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
